hero.title: ABOUT
hero.subtitle: We capture the things we are too quiet to say, and trust the frame to hold them still.

intro.name: Jaspreet
intro.openingLine: I used to think important moments would announce themselves.
quietLines: A laugh that escapes too early.
Someone looking away before they realise they're being seen.

piecesSection.subtitle: Seven small things that have very little to do with photography and everything to do with the way I see.

pieces.title: Between Pages & Perfumes
pieces.body: A second-hand bookshop and a half-empty bottle of oud — that's where most of my evenings end up.

pieces.title: A City That Knows My Story
pieces.body: Toronto has watched me grow up twice — once as a kid, once again behind a camera.

pieces.title: The Great Escape
pieces.body: A road trip with no fixed return is my favourite kind of reset. Mountains preferred.

pieces.title: Strings & Scribbles
pieces.body: A small guitar I can't really play and a notebook full of half-finished thoughts.

pieces.title: Comfort on a Plate
pieces.body: My mother's rajma-chawal could end most wars. It certainly ends most of my bad days.

pieces.title: Still on the Horizon
pieces.body: A list of places, projects and quiet ambitions that I'm in no rush to finish.

lines: And if you've made it this far,
The truth is, I still feel nervous before every shoot.
I still wonder if the light will show up.
I still get excited when a photograph becomes more than a photograph.
outro.finalLine: Maybe we'll make something worth remembering
outro.ctaText: If you've read this far, we probably already get along.
